core_mechanism: fast–slow timescale separation (adiabatic/singular-perturbation reduction) of a finite-size stochastic Markov jump process, isolating how intrinsic demographic noise selects and stabilizes long-lived metastable states among otherwise degenerate deterministic equilibria
brief_reason: The abstract resolves whether finite-population demographic noise favors one state over another by a theoretical timescale-separation analysis that characterizes noise-driven, long-lasting metastable (polymorphic) states in a system whose deterministic limit does not distinguish them.
